what:
Make `@vt-pi/agent-planner` the single source of truth for the plan-artifact types (`Plan`, `PlanTask`) and have `@vt-pi/agent-coder` import them instead of maintaining its own copy.

Concretely: (1) In `packages/agent-planner/extensions/submit-plan/logic.ts`, align the `PlanTask` interface with the actual `submit_plan` tool schema declared in `packages/agent-planner/extensions/submit-plan/index.ts` — i.e. fields are exactly `title`, `goal`, `acceptance`, `constraints`; remove the stale `files` and `specialist` fields that no longer exist in the tool's typebox parameters. Re-export `Plan` and `PlanTask` from the planner's public entry point (`packages/agent-planner/index.ts`, which is what `package.json` `exports` points at) so they are importable by package name. (2) In `@vt-pi/agent-coder`, add a `workspace:*` dependency on `@vt-pi/agent-planner` and import `Plan`/`PlanTask` from it, deleting the coder's own duplicate `Plan`/`PlanTask` interface definitions while keeping the coder-specific types (`TaskExecutionContext`, `CommitParams`) referencing the imported ones. Disregard the stray `packages/agent-coder/out/` directory entirely — do not edit or preserve it.
why: The shape of the plan artifact is currently defined independently in both packages, and the two definitions have drifted apart. The planner's `PlanTask` in `logic.ts` declares `files` and `specialist` fields that the real `submit_plan` tool schema (its typebox `parameters` in `index.ts`) does not emit, so the TypeScript type lies about what the tool actually produces, while the coder's separate copy has the correct fields. Two hand-maintained copies of the same contract that the producer emits and the consumer reads is a latent bug source: any future field change must be mirrored by hand in both places, and they're already out of sync. Centralizing the type in the producing package and importing it in the consumer removes the duplication and guarantees producer and consumer agree on one definition that matches the tool interface.

tasks.title: Align planner PlanTask with the tool schema and export plan types
tasks.goal: Make agent-planner the single, correct source of truth for the plan-artifact types. In packages/agent-planner/extensions/submit-plan/logic.ts, change the PlanTask interface to match exactly the submit_plan tool's typebox parameters in index.ts (title, goal, acceptance, constraints), removing the stale `files` and `specialist` fields that the tool no longer emits. Re-export Plan and PlanTask from the planner's public entry point (packages/agent-planner/index.ts) so consumers can import them by package name via the package's `exports`.
tasks.acceptance: PlanTask in logic.ts has exactly the fields title, goal, acceptance, constraints (no files, no specialist), matching the typebox schema in extensions/submit-plan/index.ts. Plan and PlanTask are re-exported from packages/agent-planner/index.ts. Any references in extensions/submit-plan/logic.test.ts to the removed fields are updated. `nix build` passes (planner build + tests).
tasks.constraints: Do not change the submit_plan tool's runtime behavior, validation logic, or serialization. Do not touch packages/agent-coder/out/. Keep the existing exported names Plan and PlanTask.

tasks.title: Import shared plan types in agent-coder instead of duplicating them
tasks.goal: Remove the duplicated Plan/PlanTask definitions from @vt-pi/agent-coder and consume the canonical ones from @vt-pi/agent-planner. Add a `workspace:*` dependency on @vt-pi/agent-planner to the coder's package.json, and update the coder's plan-consuming modules to import Plan and PlanTask from the planner package, deleting the coder's own Plan/PlanTask interface declarations. Keep coder-specific types (TaskExecutionContext, CommitParams) in the coder, now referencing the imported Plan/PlanTask.
tasks.acceptance: @vt-pi/agent-coder no longer declares its own Plan or PlanTask interfaces; it imports them from @vt-pi/agent-planner. package.json declares @vt-pi/agent-planner as a workspace:* dependency and the lockfile is updated. Coder-specific types TaskExecutionContext and CommitParams remain defined in the coder. `nix build` passes.
tasks.constraints: Ignore and do not modify or preserve packages/agent-coder/out/. Preserve the coder's runtime behavior (prompt building, commit-task tool). Follow the interface with no `files`/`specialist` fields.
